title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

description: Experience the true taste of Liguria with this authentic Focaccia Genovese. This beloved Italian flatbread boasts a perfectly crisp, golden crust and a soft, airy interior, generously infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil. It's an unforgettable culinary journey to the heart of Genoa, right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.notes: plus 2 tablespoons for pan and topping

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, granulated sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently to dissolve the sugar and yeast, then let it sit for 5-10 minutes until a foamy layer appears on the surface, indicating the yeast is active and ready.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Drizzle in ¼ cup of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a sturdy wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, cohesive dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean large bowl with a little olive oil. Place the kneaded dough into the bowl, turning it once to coat all sides with oil. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise in a warm, draft-free place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the dough has doubled significantly in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Second Rise
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough to release the air. Drizzle 2 tablespoons of olive oil into a 9x13 inch baking pan and spread it evenly across the bottom and sides. Transfer the dough to the prepared pan, turning it once to coat it in the oil. Gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to tear it. Cover the pan again and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Dimple and Top
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough, pressing almost to the bottom of the pan. Drizzle generously with the remaining extra virgin olive oil. Evenly sprinkle the chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt over the top of the focaccia.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Place the baking pan in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is beautifully golden brown on top and cooked through. If the top starts to brown too quickly, you can loosely tent it with aluminum foil. Remove from the oven, let it cool slightly in the pan, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing and serving.

tips: For truly authentic dimples, use only your fingertips and press firmly, almost to the bottom of the pan. This creates the characteristic airy texture and pockets for olive oil.
Always use good quality extra virgin olive oil; it is a star ingredient in Focaccia Genovese and greatly impacts the flavor. Fresh rosemary is also non-negotiable for the best aroma and taste.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh, warm from the oven. Store leftovers at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, or freeze slices for longer storage. Reheat gently in the oven.
article:
Unlock the secrets to a beloved Italian classic that captivates with its rustic charm and irresistible aroma: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. This isn't just any bread; it's a testament to the simplicity and richness of Italian baking, a flatbread that whispers tales of Ligurian sunshine and generations of culinary passion. Imagine a golden, dimpled surface, glistening with extra virgin olive oil, adorned with fragrant rosemary sprigs and coarse sea salt, yielding to an interior that's airy, chewy, and utterly addictive. If you're ready to bring a piece of Italy into your kitchen, this comprehensive guide will walk you through crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary, a bread that’s perfect for any occasion.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?
Focaccia Genovese, or *Fügassa* in the local Ligurian dialect, is the quintessential flatbread of Genoa, a coastal city in the Italian region of Liguria. Unlike its thicker, often more bread-like cousins found elsewhere in Italy, the Genovese version is distinctively thin, remarkably light, and incredibly tender. Its hallmark is a generous application of high-quality extra virgin olive oil, both in the dough and drizzled lavishly on top before baking. The characteristic dimples, pressed into the dough with fingertips, serve a crucial purpose: they create small pools for the olive oil and act as reservoirs for the salt, ensuring every bite is bursting with flavor and a delightful textural contrast.

Historically, focaccia was considered a humble food, often baked in the ashes of a fire by sailors and fishermen. Over time, it evolved into the sophisticated yet simple staple we know today, enjoyed as a breakfast item, a snack, or an accompaniment to meals. The addition of fresh rosemary is a classic enhancement, infusing the bread with its piney, aromatic notes that perfectly complement the fruity olive oil and the subtle sweetness of the dough. When you bite into a piece of authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary, you're experiencing centuries of culinary tradition.

## Ingredients You Need for Focaccia Genovese
The beauty of Focaccia Genovese lies in its minimalist ingredient list, where quality truly shines. Opt for the best you can find, especially when it comes to flour and olive oil.

*   **All-Purpose Flour (or "00" Flour):** Approximately 500g. "00" flour is traditional for Italian baking, offering a finer texture, but good quality all-purpose flour works wonderfully.
*   **Warm Water:** Around 350-400ml. The exact amount can vary based on flour type and humidity; aim for a soft, pliable dough.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (one standard packet).
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g for the dough, plus extra coarse sea salt for sprinkling on top.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** 50-70ml for the dough, plus a generous amount (at least 50ml) for drizzling and coating the pan. This is non-negotiable for authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** A few sprigs, leaves stripped and roughly chopped, plus small sprigs for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Focaccia Genovese
Creating this masterpiece is a process, but a rewarding one. Patience is key!

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in 50ml of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
3.  **Knead:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 10-15 minutes until it's smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. If using a stand mixer with a dough hook, knead on medium speed for 8-10 minutes. The dough should be soft.
4.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Lightly grease a large bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Liberally coat a 9x13 inch (or similar size) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil. Don't be shy; this oil contributes to the focaccia's crispy bottom crust.
6.  **Shape the Dough:** Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan. It might resist; if so, let it rest for 10-15 minutes, then continue stretching.
7.  **Second Rise & Dimpling:** Once the dough roughly fills the pan, cover it again and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes. After this, uncover and generously drizzle more olive oil over the surface. Now, with oiled fingertips, press deep dimples all over the dough, ensuring they go almost to the bottom of the pan. This is characteristic of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary.
8.  **Add Toppings:** Sprinkle the chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt evenly over the dimpled surface. You can also press in a few small rosemary sprigs for visual appeal.
9.  **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and beautifully crisp on the bottom.
10. **Cool and Serve:** Once baked, immediately transfer the focaccia to a wire rack. Drizzle with a final touch of high-quality extra virgin olive oil while warm. Let it cool slightly before slicing and serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Focaccia Genovese Results
Achieving focaccia perfection is within reach with these expert tips:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is Paramount:** This cannot be stressed enough. A good extra virgin olive oil isn't just a cooking fat; it's a flavor component. Its fruity, peppery notes are integral to the authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary experience.
*   **Hydration is Key:** Focaccia dough is a high-hydration dough, meaning it's quite wet and sticky. Don't be tempted to add too much extra flour during kneading. A sticky dough makes for a tender crumb.
*   **Gentle Handling:** After the first rise, handle the dough very gently to avoid deflating it too much. You want to preserve those precious air bubbles.
*   **Deep Dimples:** Don't be afraid to press those dimples deep! They are essential for capturing olive oil and creating pockets of intense flavor.
*   **Preheat the Oven Thoroughly:** A hot oven ensures a rapid rise and a beautifully crisp crust.
*   **Don't Overbake:** Keep an eye on the color. You want a golden-brown top and a nicely browned bottom. Overbaking can dry it out.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Rosemary Focaccia
The versatility of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary makes it a perfect companion for a multitude of dishes.

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve it simply with a bowl of good olive oil for dipping, perhaps with a sprinkle of balsamic glaze. It's also fantastic alongside a platter of cured meats (prosciutto, salami) and cheeses (parmesan, pecorino).
*   **With Meals:** It makes an excellent side bread for soups, stews, or pasta dishes, perfect for soaking up rich sauces.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally to create unique, flavorful sandwiches. Fill with fresh mozzarella, tomatoes, basil, and a drizzle of pesto, or with grilled vegetables and goat cheese.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch:** Many Italians enjoy focaccia for breakfast, perhaps with a strong coffee. It's also delightful with scrambled eggs or alongside a frittata.

## Variations and Substitutions for Focaccia Genovese
While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is a masterpiece on its own, you can certainly experiment with variations:

*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you could try fresh oregano, thyme, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Olives:** Black or green olives, pitted and roughly chopped, can be pressed into the dough along with the rosemary.
*   **Cherry Tomatoes:** Halved cherry tomatoes, pressed into the dimples, add a burst of sweetness and color.
*   **Onions:** Thinly sliced red onions, caramelized slightly before baking or raw, can be a delicious topping.
*   **Garlic:** Finely minced garlic can be added to the olive oil drizzle before baking, or even infused into the olive oil used in the dough.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While not traditional, gluten-free all-purpose flour blends can be used, though the texture will differ. Look for blends designed for yeast breads.
*   **Whole Wheat:** Substituting a portion of the all-purpose flour with whole wheat flour will add a nuttier flavor and denser texture. Start with replacing 25% of the white flour.

Baking Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is more than just making bread; it's an experience, a journey into the heart of Italian culinary tradition. The aroma filling your kitchen will be intoxicating, and the taste will transport you straight to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast. So roll up your sleeves, embrace the stickiness, and prepare to delight in one of Italy's most cherished flatbreads. Buon appetito!
